Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
The Lord upholdeth all that fall, and raiseth up all those that be bowed down.
Thou hast thrust sore at me that I might fall: but the Lord helped me.

“Strengthen the Feeble Knees” By Elder Marvin J. Ashton Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles There is a phrase used four times in the standard works which has always intrigued me. It is the expression “feeble knees.” By definition, feeble means weak, not strong, without force, easily broken, frail. When Frederick G. Williams was called to be a Counselor to Joseph Smith, he was given this charge:…
Wherefore, be faithful; stand in the office which I have appointed unto you; succor the weak, lift up the hands which hang down, and strengthen the feeble knees.
¶ Strengthen ye the weak hands, and confirm the feeble knees.
All hands shall be feeble, and all knees shall be weak as water.

I never believed that the righteous have ever suffered as much as the
wicked. 11:274.
But thou shalt say unto them, This is a nation that obeyeth not the voice of the Lord their God, nor receiveth correction: truth is perished, and is cut off from their mouth.

And if the righteous scarcely be saved, where shall the ungodly and the sinner appear?

And the channels of the sea appeared, the foundations of the world were discovered, at the rebuking of the Lord, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.

And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were gathered together, the floods stood upright as an heap, and the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.

For the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the power of God.
